entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 4 - Mobile device capabilities
entry.content:
Couple of holes I fell into:

  • I used the id field instead of the correct fsq_id field.
  • My photo API was working but the images were not displayed, and only after I specified an exact width did they appear.
  • If you do not see any network activity, then make sure you have a flow function actually making the call -- this is very curious you do not have an API call. If you have to add one manually, do so.
  • I copied by get record collection and set data variable flow functions from data pane to page logic pane, but hen the output for the set data variable is no longer valid, and I forgot for a while to reset this to the output for the current page's get record collection.
  • Also, briefly, for some reason I cannot explain, my data variable had the places API fields when I went to bind data items in repeat.

Just take it step by step in order to debug, for example:

  1. Does the data resource Test work?
  2. Is the data variable running flow function and returning data?
  3. Is the UI component bound properly?
  4. ...

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Work Zone: Integrate SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud content to SAP Mobile Start
entry.content:
Hi @Amith_Nair ,

Thanks for such detailed information. We followed the steps and we are now able to get the roles and the role specific tiles in BWZ site. The users are successfully authenticated to login with our identity provider. But, when we click on the tiles, it's again asking for user credentials; and no credentials are working here.

Any idea, what might have gone wrong here?

Regards,

Anupam

entry.title: Re: 3 ways to build a website on a tight budget
entry.content:
Your explanation about making a website was so clear! It's like you said, websites are just like online business cards. They're super important for showing people who you are and what you do, whether you're selling stuff or offering services.

I totally get what you mean about SAP Consultants having blogs to show off their skills. And your WordPress site sounds awesome too! It's like having a digital portfolio to show off to anyone who wants to know more about you and your work. Thanks for breaking it down, man!

entry.title: Managers can now create Employee Central Quick Actions for Positions
entry.content:
With the 1H 2024 Release, On the Company Info page, the My Team Positions page can be used to manage and update the positions by managers or supervisors. With the new My Team Position feature you can now use templates to create Quick actions for positions for managers

My team position is available under quick actions on the home page and is available upon activating the Role-based permissions for My team position. You can enable it as follows -

Administrator Permissions--> Manage Business Configuration -->Employee Central Quick Action Template permission.

 

Once the permissions are enabled my team positions will be available for managers under company info.

 

Under quick actions

 

 

Once these available templates are configured. The following templates are available –

 

  1. Create Same-Level Position (Copy Rule)
  2. Create Same-Level Position (Copy All Fields)
  3. Change Location of Position
  4. Change Job Details of Position
  5. Change Organizational Data of Position

 

To configure the template, go to Manager data --> create --> select employee central quick action template. Select the use case. Here I have selected “Change Organizational Data of Position”.

Provide quick Action name. I have given “Change Organizational Data of Position”

 

Select the position information as follows –

 

Base object – Position

Field – Department, division, business unit, company, cost center. You can select up to 8 fields. Save.

 

 

 

The permission above quick action under User permissions à Employee Central Quick Actions

 

 

 

Now login as a manager or proxy as a manager. Following Action will be available under my Team positions.

 

 

Managers can now click on this action to change Department, division, business unit, company, or cost center and synchronize with Job information on people profile.

entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 4 - Mobile device capabilities
entry.content:
Thank you @YogSSohanee & @Dan_Wroblewski for the help. As it turns out, I didn't maintain the fsq_id for the Photos API (for the Details API it was maintained) 🙈

Regarding part 2 : I manage to return the correct distance (I see that from an Alert in the Logic Canvas), however, the description is not getting updated with the updated page variable. I guess I'll have to do a Javascript to update that particular text

entry.title: How to post code in SAP Community >=2024
entry.content:
I'm posting a question with code, but the code looks ugly, no color and no monospaced font.

How to make it display pretty?

e.g. Want to download PDF from Spool using 'CONVERT_ABA... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: How to post code in SAP Community >=2024
entry.content:
First click on "..." (Expand toolbar):

Then position your cursor where you want to insert your code and and click "</>" (Insert/Edit code sample):

Select the code language, paste your code and click OK:

Now the code is displayed with color and monospaced font:

Note that if you edit your post again, there is a bug about blank lines added before and after the code, SAP is informed and it will be fixed → Blog editor does strange changes - SAP Community (last update: April 24th, 2024).

entry.title: Re: Want to download PDF from Spool using 'CONVERT_ABAPSPOOLJOB_2_PDF'
entry.content:
Please format your code nicely. See Solved: How to post code in SAP Community >=2024 - SAP Community.

e.g. see how it looks after:

CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY'
    EXPORTING
      buffer     = lv_xstring
    TABLES
      binary_tab = data_tab.

Nhat

entry.title: ERMS - What is the xpath expression when creating a new attribute?
entry.content:
Hello together, when creating a new attribute for the rule modeler it has to be provided an XPATH Expression: 

Can we choose any xpath we want or how to identify the correct XPATH for the new attribute?

entry.title: Re: Want to download PDF from Spool using 'CONVERT_ABAPSPOOLJOB_2_PDF'
entry.content:
First of all, there are several types of spool requests, ABAP List, OTF (SAPScript Form, Smart Form), Adobe PDF, etc.

CONVERT_ABAPSPOOLJOB_2_PDF is only for spool requests of type ABAP List.

If you use PDF_DESTINATION = 'X', only the parameter BIN_FILE is filled (not PDF_BYTECOUNT and not PDF).

One possible solution (among others): don't use PDF_DESTINATION (same as passing it = space) which will fill the parameters PDF_BYTECOUNT and PDF, declare LTA_LX_PDF of type SOLIX_TAB (lines with one component of type X and length 256), no need of SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY, download with BIN_FILESIZE = lv_bin_size and DATA_TAB = lta_lx_pdf.

entry.title: Step Back in Time with SAP Inside Track Belgium 2024!
entry.content:
Hello SAP enthusiasts!

We are thrilled to announce that the next SAP Inside Track event will take place on Saturday, September 21, 2024, at the Arηs Group offices located at Ikaroslaan 53, 1930 Zaventem, Belgium. This year, we're taking a delightful journey back in time with our theme: Old Skool.

 

What to Expect

As always, prepare to dive into the latest and greatest of what SAP has to offer.  What is special about this year's edition is that we will also honour the legacy of SAP and include some references to the Old Skool way of doing things. 

Why use a webservice if you can download a file, write it to a floppy and physically deliver the info, right? 

We are currently building our agenda and would love to hear from you. Stay tuned for our upcoming call for speakers!

A Little Fun Along the Way

Get ready for a nostalgic twist with our SAP pop quiz! Test your knowledge of SAP’s history and maybe even surprise yourself with how much you know about the origins of R/3 and R/2.

A Big Thank You to Arηs

We would like to extend a heartfelt thank you to Arηs Group for hosting us. Their support makes it possible to bring our community together and share our passion for SAP technologies in a fantastic setting.

For those who are wondering what the η character signifies: Arηs (/aris/) stands for “Advanced and Reliable Information Systems”. Eta (η) is the seventh letter of the Greek alphabet pronounced /i/, and translated into H in the Latin alphabet. 

(Might come in handy at a pop quiz... who knows 🤭)

Join Us!

Don’t miss out on this unique opportunity to connect with fellow SAP professionals, share experiences, and gain insights. You can subscribe to the event here. Spots are limited, so make sure to secure yours soon!

More information

More information on the detailed agenda will be made available through the website and twitter.

We can't wait to see you there and explore the nostalgic world of SAP together. Mark your calendars and get ready for a blast from the past that promises to be both enlightening and fun!

Looking forward to seeing you there and making this year's edition a memorable one!!

entry.title: Find busiest CarrierId based on FlightDate using SAP CDS View
entry.content:
Hi Experts ,
I need to get the below expected output. Let me explain: as 07.04 CarrierId has highest number of trip that is 4 so it should be the output instead of 10.02. All other entries have single entry based on date so it will be in output.

Input data:

CarrierID Connection ID FlightDate Price
UA175010.02.2024250
UA189007.04.2024450
UA276007.04.2024150
UA197007.04.2024100
AA349011.04.2024340
AA196013.04.2024560
SQ349015.05.2014120

 

Expected output:

CarrierID FlightDate
UA07.04.2024
AA11.04.2024
AA13.04.2024
SQ15.05.2014

I tried to create two CDS Views to solve the issue but I can only get the busiest CarrierId without date in output. But requirement is to display the date with CarrierID.

I tried to find output with the below views:

  1. define view entity View1 as select from /dmo/booking  
{
 key carrier_id as CarrierId,
     flight_date as FlightDate,     
  count(*) as FlightCount   
}
group by
 carrier_id,flight_date
  2. define view entity View2 as select from View1
{
 key CarrierId as CarrierId,
     FlightDate as FlightDate,
 max(FlightCount) as Numberofflight
}group by CarrierId

    This view is not working as we need to add non-aggregated fields in the Group By clause but if I add FlightDate in the Group By then the output will be different.

entry.title: Vendor Subrange
entry.content:
Vendor Subrange for order currency.

Let’s consider we have one vendor i.e. “XYZ LTD.” which is maintained for
some specific Purchase Organization “CP00” for Order currency “INR”

But the same vendor can also accept multiple currency like INR, USD, EUR, & AED
etc.  

We can assign multiple order currency for single plant also and for one vendor.
So for that we need to maintain the vendor order currency for three different
countries for this purpose SAP define the Vendor Sub-Range & Can assign
respective Ordered currency & Partner function each one of those.

Create a New Vendor Mater Record by using T-Code: - XK01

Under the Purchasing view click on Different data as shown below.

By click on this system will give below message

Click on YES

Enter the Vendor Subrange & VSR Description.
➔ Enter the Plant & Tick on Purchasing Data.
➔ Press enter.

Enter the Order Currency & Terms of Payment.

Again create Another Vendor Sub Range for another currency as below.

Enter the Order Currency & Terms of Payment. For Second VSR.

Save the data.

By display the Vendor by using T-Code: - XK03 check the VSR created as
below.

Same vendor number but we assigned multiple order currencies.

While creating Purchase order we can use supplier subrange to choose the required currency 

As per this we can set the Multiple Ordered Currency for Single Plant & well as for Different Plant & same Vendor also.

All the remaining procedure is Same as P2P cycle.

Thankyou.

entry.title: How to ask a question in SAP Community >= 2024
entry.content: I'm a little bit lost in the SAP Community, what is the right way to ask a question or find a solution to my question.

entry.title: Re: How to ask a question in SAP Community >= 2024
entry.content:
It's officially explained here: How do I ask a question - Help - SAP Community.

Choose the adequate group:

Choose the Q&A board in the selected group (here, it was the Technology group):

You may then enter the title of your question in order to see other similar questions and check their answers. If they don't answer your question, try to refine your question, and click Continue only if you have found nothing:

Do not immediately click on Continue. First, click any of the proposed answers to see their details:

You may right-click "More about this topic" and then "Open link in new tab" to see the details of all answers and comments in another tab:

If there's nothing which answers your question, then click Continue to create your own question.

Additionally, pay attention to post in a Q&A group (e.g. developer/programming questions should be posted in Technology Q&A) excluding SAP Websites Q&A (it's reserved to questions about sap.com Websites like e.g SAP Community usage, SAP for Me (SAP Support), SAP Library, SAP Developer Center, etc.) If you don't post to a Q&A group (i.e. if you post in a Discussion group like e.g. the Application Development group, or if you post a comment), you won't be able to mark the good answer as The Solution and so future visitors won't find it easily. NB: you can't move a Discussion to a Q&A group, you can only recreate the question in a Q&A group.

If you post some code, please be nice with your readers by using the beautify feature: Solved: How to post code in SAP Community >=2024 - SAP Community.

entry.title: Re: S4H - the transfer of simulation version into the act...
entry.content: Hi Caetano, I was expecting the following when we copy the planned orders from the simulation version: the planned orders will be copied 1:1 and hence they will create secondary demand for the components. But very often I see that planned orders are copied but no secondary demand is created for the components

entry.title: RFC to receive PDF files
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I'm working on a scenario which is related to SFTP to RFC. Where I need to pass pdf files from a SFTP server to RFC. Please let me know which type to RFC destination(Example:- ABAP, HTTP Connection to External Server, Internal Connection, TCP/IP Connection) and function module I've to create so that it can receive pdf files.

Thanks and Regards,
Sufiyan.

entry.title: Fixing vehicle resource in the transportation lane
entry.content:
Hi,

How can I set-up the following in S/4 HANA TM : I need to check if the resource X can be fixed on the particular lane so that during Optimizer run the system will pick resource X only.

entry.title: Re: Using Integration Suite API's with Basic Auth
entry.content:
Hi @Robin_Walther 

The way you've described it, it should work.

This error can occur when you provide a wrong password, so please double-check that. Also please note that due to caching mechanisms in SAP Cloud Integration, it can take up to 1 hour until role changes are considered.

Kind regards,
Daniil

entry.title: BUSINESS PARTNER
entry.content:
BUSINESS PARTNER

 

A business partner represents the highest-level object in an SAP S/4HANA system.

A Business Partner can be a person, organization, group of people, or a group of organizations.

 

 

 

BP Configuration:

Create Account Group (OBD3)

PATH: SPRO->SAP Reference IMG->Financial Accounting->Accounts Receivable and Payable->Vendor Accounts->Master Data->Preparation of creating vendor master data->Define account groups with screen layout (vendors)

 

Create Number Range for Account Group (XKN1)

PATH: SPRO->SAP Reference IMG->Financial Accounting->Accounts Receivable and Payable->Vendor Accounts->Master Data->Preparation of creating vendor master data->Create number ranges for vendor accounts

 

Assign Number Range to account groups (OBAS)

PATH: SPRO->SAP Reference IMG->Financial Accounting->Accounts Receivable and Payable->Vendor Accounts->Master Data->Preparation of creating vendor master data->Assign Number range to vendor account groups

 

 

Define Business Partner Number Range (BUCF)

PATH:  SPRO->SAP Reference IMG->Cross Application Components->SAP Business Partner->Business Partner- >Basic Settings->Number Ranges and Groupings->Define number ranges

 

 

Assign Business Partner Number range to Grouping

PATH: SPRO->SAP Reference IMG->Cross Application Components->SAP Business Partner->Business Partner- >Basic Settings->Number Ranges and Groupings->Define Groupings and assign number ranges

 

Link Grouping and Account Group

 PATH: SPRO->SAP Reference IMG->Cross Application Components->Master Data Synchronization-   >Custom/Vendor integration->Business Partner Settings->Settings for vendor integration->Field assignment for vendor integration->Assign Keys->Define number assignment for direction BP to vendor

BUSINESS PARTNER ROLE CONFIGURATION

 BP Role:

SPRO->SAP Reference IMG->Cross Application Components->SAP Business Partner->Business Partner- >Basic Settings->Business Partner Roles->Define BP Roles and Define BP Role Groupings

 

Create BP : T CODE - BP

entry.title: Re: In-House Repair in S/4HANA Service - Start with an Overview

@Udo , Hope all is well!

Appreciate your insights in config steps or process steps. That could help me to complete this End to End Inhouse process. 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: RFC to receive PDF files
entry.content: Could you clarify the scenario, where do you want to create the RFC destination, where do you want to create the ABAP program, etc.? <PDF file in server> ----via-SFTP---> ABAP system 1? ----via-RFC----> ABAP system 2?

entry.title: Clean Core within SAP Sales and Service Cloud extensions
entry.content:
The first wave of CRMs helped tidy up customer data and processes, but they didn't really make the customer experience much better. Nowadays, companies of all sizes are focusing on improving customers experience because it's been proven that happy customers are more likely to come back. If their experiences aren't memorable, they might choose a competitor next time.

Because of this, businesses are always brainstorming new ideas to make their products and services even better. But improving things like system stability and scalability is essential for these changes to work smoothly.

In the past, companies could tweak (through exits, BAdIs or, even by copying standard objects) their SAP systems however they wanted because they were usually kept in their own data centers. But things changed when SAP shifted to a cloud-based approach. Now, all Sales/Service Cloud customers share the same SAP baseline, so changes can't be handled any further as they were made in the past. That's where the Clean Core approach comes in—it's a new way of thinking about extending SAP's capabilities specifically for the cloud era.

Understanding the Clean Core Concept

Clean Core revolves around maintaining a clear separation between core functionalities and customizations, with the aim of achieving a modern, flexible, and cloud-compliant system. In the realm of SAP CX extensions, this translates to safeguarding the integrity of the core SAP CX products while allowing for tailored enhancements to meet specific business needs.

Think of it as organizing a toolbox where each tool serves for a unique purpose without clutter. Similarly, a Clean Core architecture ensures that the essential functionalities (for example, customer data management, order processing, or marketing automation) remain untouched by customizations. This not only ensures system stability but also facilitates seamless upgrades and future scalability.

Benefits of a Clean Core Approach

Embracing the Clean Core concept offers several advantages for businesses leveraging SAP CX extensions:

  1. Streamlined upgrades: By keeping customizations separate from the core SAP CX platform, businesses can streamline the upgrade process, minimizing downtime and reducing the risk of compatibility issues.
  2. Enhanced agility: A Clean Core architecture empowers businesses to respond quickly to changing market demands and customer expectations. With the ability to innovate without compromising system integrity, organizations can stay ahead in today's competitive landscape.
  3. Improved performance: Removing unnecessary customizations from the core SAP CX platform can lead to enhanced system performance, responsiveness, continuity and stability, resulting in a smoother customer experience.
  4. Simplifying the way of working: Promoting the fit-to-standard compliance with clean core modular innovation makes customers to use “out of the box” SAP solutions and add their additional capabilities at the desired pace.

Additionally, it could be pointed out the fact of making reduction in the TCO (Total Cost of Ownership) by using infrastructure and licenses in a much more efficient way.

Extensibility within Clean Core approach

Clean Core Extensibility is basically a way of extending SAP systems without messing with the core application. It's all about keeping your custom enhancements separate from the main SAP setup, so you can update your system without causing any issues. In simpler terms, when you add extra features or tweaks, they won't interfere with the basic functions of SAP that keep your business running smoothly.

For Sales and Service Cloud products, there are in-app (built-in) tools that help you customize things without getting too technical. But for more complex changes, you might need to consider side-by-side extensions. The In-App tools in v1 are mostly UI related. In terms of more complex extensions for v1, the way to go is through PDI development which has caused (to me) many issues. This PDI developments are introduced through a programming language called ABSL (ABAP Scripting Language) which, for being direct on my words, has nothing to do with ABAP and it is implemented through an SDK. Also, coding this has lots of restrictions in comparison to the coding we were used to develop those who have invested years with ABAP.

In v2, in-app extensibility is limited to fields and can't create full custom objects as possible in v1. So this could be solved by implementing a RAP or CAP solution in BTP and connecting it into the v2, for example, through a Mashup.

The concept of clean core extensibility can be distilledin this framework of best practices that a customer adopts when implementing SAP Sales/Service Cloud products:

  • Adopting a policy of zero modifications
  • Eliminating enhancements that are redundant to standard code and functionality and also eliminating copies of SAP objects
  • Using released APIs only ("upgrade-stable interfaces")
  • Leveraging the key user (in-app) extensibility to its full extent
  • Employing the capabilities and services offered by SAP BTP to build larger extension applications
  • Using SAP Integration Suite

Best Practices for Implementing a Clean Core

Implementing a Clean Core approach within SAP CX extensions requires careful planning and execution. Here are some best practices:

  1. Modularization: Break down complex functionalities into modular components, allowing for greater flexibility and easier maintenance.
  2. Encapsulation: Protect core functionalities from direct modification by encapsulating them within well-defined interfaces and abstraction layers.
  3. Version Control: Implement robust version control mechanisms to manage customizations effectively and track changes over time.

Real-World use cases

Over the past decade, I've worked on several projects implementing C4C (now known as Sales/Service Cloud v1) where I tackled various challenging requirements. Looking back, if I would face similar tasks now, regardless of whether it's a v1 or v2 product, I'd opt for a side-by-side extension approach.

For instance, on a Service Cloud project, a client needed a feature where a main ticket would automatically close once all its sub-tickets were marked as 'Closed'. Surprisingly, neither v1 nor v2 had this feature when I last checked.

My approach today (for v2) would involve setting up a trigger using an Autoflow, which activates whenever a sub-ticket is closed. This trigger would then call a service on SAP's Business Technology Platform (BTP), developed using CAP. This service would then check if all sub-tickets are closed and, if so, it would also close the main ticket.

Also, my approach today (for v1) would involve setting up a trigger using Workflow Rules, which calls a PDI action that ends up calling almost the same CAP development from BTP. Although logic would be the same, the only adjustment inside CAP would consist on calling different endpoints.

However, when I encountered this requirement previously, the solution we implemented was a bit different. We created a Workflow Rule triggered when a ticket reached 'Closed' status. This rule would then execute an action developed using PDI, which would check if all sub-tickets were closed and decide whether to close the main ticket. This approach could have been tweaked by integrating the Workflow Rule directly into the PDI solution, triggered by an AfterSave event.

Closing comments

One of the big challenges we face is figuring out how to transition the army of tech experts who've been immersed in SAP CRM technologies like WebUI, BOL, and GenIL for decades into SAP CAP/RAP developers. This is the challenge that really gets me thinking and motivates me to take action.

I think the key lies in finding a balance between employees and employers. It's about consultants being proactive and open to learning new ways of doing things, while companies need to step up and offer opportunities for their teams to not just learn, but actually put their new skills into practice.

What do you think about this? Are you willing to jump into this new Era? Have you already done it? Do you agree with Clean Core principles? Have you already worked with this side-by-side context within SAP CX? I am interested to read your experiences, don't be shy!

entry.title: how to combine/simplify these multiple IF Statements?
entry.content:
I have below multiple IF statements which I want to see combined,
I tried using ELSEIF and CASE but its not applicable here because the comparison operator is <> instead of =

Below is the code:

 

        IF <ls_load>-atnam <> 'AA'.
          IF VALUE #( lt_num[ charact = 'AA' ]-value_from OPTIONAL ) IS INITIAL.
            DELETE lt_num WHERE charact = 'AA'.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.

        IF <ls_load>-atnam <> 'BB'.
          IF VALUE #( lt_num[ charact = 'BB' ]-value_from OPTIONAL ) IS INITIAL.
            DELETE lt_num WHERE charact = 'BB'.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.

        IF <ls_load>-atnam <> 'CC'.
          IF VALUE #( lt_num[ charact = 'CC' ]-value_from OPTIONAL ) IS INITIAL.
            DELETE lt_num WHERE charact = 'CC'.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.

entry.title: sapjco3: How to add certificate for SNC connection
entry.content:
Hi experts!

I am trying to set up an SNC connection to my SAP Netweaver system, using SAP Jco in Java (Ubuntu).

I have managed to make an SNC connection with SAP GUI, so the SNC infrastructure should be mostly in place. I am unsure how to add the SNC certificate to the SAP Jco, because, I am getting this error when I try to run my Java program: 

ERROR       GSS-API(maj): No credentials were supplied
            Unable to establish the security context
            target="p:CN=NPL"

Can the certificate be linked by adding some property here?

sapProperties.setProperty(DestinationDataProvider.JCO_SNC_MODE, "1");
sapProperties.setProperty(DestinationDataProvider.JCO_SNC_PARTNERNAME, "p:CN=NPL");
sapProperties.setProperty(DestinationDataProvider.JCO_SNC_MYNAME, "p:CN=NPL");
sapProperties.setProperty(DestinationDataProvider.JCO_SNC_QOP, "1");

Help is much appreciated!

entry.title: Generate add-ons .ARD file
entry.content:
Hello!

I know that SAP has discontinued the B1DE tool for creating add-ons in the .ARD extension. Additionally, I'm aware that SAP also recommends that add-ons be registered as LW (Light Weight), but my question is: Is it still possible to generate add-ons in the .ARD extension nowadays?

I believe it was only possible through the B1DE tool, right? Now that it's no longer available, can we still generate the .ARD installer?

If someone could send me a link to install B1DE, I would appreciate it!

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: On Uploading PDF or PPt File Getting 403 Error Forbidden
entry.content: So what does the body say, what can you deduce from the headers, and what is "its" IP address? That should tell you, where the problem is.

entry.title: VSR Optimizer considering net weight for maximum utilization instead of gross weight
entry.content: Hi, I'm using S/4 HANA TM so in VSR Optimizer as well, the system is considering net weight instead of gross weight. We have done the package building as well and the FUBR is with split quantity as 1FU is equivalent to 5 pallets. Can you please suggest if there is any setting done to have gross weight considered for calculating maximum utilization?

entry.title: Configure cTMS in combination with CHARM and CI/CD
entry.content:
Introduction

The BTP Cloud Transport Management Service (cTMS) is mostly used in combination with the CI/CD service (or any other non-SAP CI/CD tool) when you need to transport applications from one Sub Account to another Sub Account. This combination is well documented in the SAP help documentation. CI/CD deploys to your DEV Sub Account and uploads the MTAR of your application to cTMS. From here, cTMS taks over to deploy to ACC and PRD Sub Accounts. You can find the details in the documentation here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/continuous-integration-and-delivery/sap-continuous-integration-and-delivery/configure-sap-fiori-in-cloud-foundry-environment-job-in-your-repository#(optional)-integrate-sap-cloud-transport-management-into-your-pipeline

 

Besides integration with CI/CD, you want to integrate cTMS with your existing transporting system, which is for most customers Charm. This setup allows you to transport ABAP objects and BTP applications together. You could also consider using ALM as transporting system but at the moment this has not all the functionalities that you have in Charm today.

Connecting cTMS nodes to Charm require enabling the configuration “Controlled By SAP Solution Manager”:

When you enable this, you might face some of the problems listed below and the solution might might be helping you to have a clean cTMS landscape integrated with Charm.

Problems

  • DEV node in cTMS without destination cannot be saved

  • Setting a destination doesn’t make sense as the CI/CD service already deploys to the DEV Sub Account but it allows you to continue with the configuration. 
  • The flow described for Cloud ALM cannot be applied exactly the same way when using Charm as it does not require to enable “Controlled by SAP Solution Manager”, see documentation. 
    • https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/cloud-transport-management

  • All cTMS Nodes after DEV need to be controller by SAP Solution Manager if you want to integrate it with Charm BUT import to cTMS Nodes “Controlled By SAP Solution Manager” is not possible. This will cause an error in the logs of cTMS 

  • Uploading to the DEV Node in cTMS only forwards to ACC after successful import. Importing/Deploying to DEV via cTMS is not needed as this is already done by the CI/CD service
    • This is only causing overhead which is not needed
  • This means that you need to upload to cTMS Node for ACC BTP Sub Account BUT this is not possible because it is controlled by SAP Solution Manager

Solution

  • Make the DEV nodea Virtual Node
  • In SAP CI/CD, use export from “DEV-NODE” instead of "Upload to":
    • This will upload the mtar to cTMS and immediately forward it from DEV to ACC so it ends up in the queue of ACC.

 

  • In any other CI/CD tool, use the export api: “/v2/nodes/export”
    • https://api.sap.com/api/TMS_v2/path/NODE_EXPORT_BY_NAME_V2
    • See blog post with an example: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/integrate-ci-cd-with-ctms-as-part-of-sap-cloud-alm/ba-p/13653010 

In the end, DEV is a virtual node and all others are controlled by Solution Manager

Configuration of the DEV Node:

  • Upload to Node is allowed
  • Node is flagged as a virtual node

 

Configuration of all nodes after DEV:

  • “Conntrolled By SAP Solution Manager” enabled

 

Overview

In the end, your transport landscape with CI/CD, cTMS and Charm will look like this:

  • Developers will push their code from BAS or VSCode to GIT
  • GIT will trigger CI/CD through a webhook
  • CI/CD will
    • deploy to the DEV Sub Account
    • export to cTMS dev node
  • cTMS will move the transport to the queue of ACC
  • From here it can be transported using Charm

I used a separate Sub Account for the CI/CD service but this could be as well in the DEV Sub Account.

For the integration with Charm I recommend following this blog post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-use-the-integration-of-sap-cloud-platform-transport-management-into/ba-p/13443259

entry.title: Re: Re-Recording in Producer
entry.content:
Hi,

I think it will be much faster to re-record the Simulations again with the correct screensize.  Attempting to manually adjust the size of images and the location of hotspots will take way longer than just recording again.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Cannot generate a PDF after latest Microsoft Office u...
entry.content:
Hi Ankur,

Generation of documentation only works for Enable Now when Office 365 is installed on the authoring PC; not the browser version of the application.  I suspect that you are trying to generate documentation with a cloud version of Office 365.  The following details are in the Enable Now requirements document.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors Talent Intelligence Hub H1 2024 Release Insights
entry.content:
SAP SuccessFactors continues to set the bar high with its first half of 2024 release, particularly in the Talent Intelligence Hub and Succession & Development. This release packs exciting new features and essential updates that will transform how organisations manage and develop their workforce. Here’s an integrated overview of what’s new and noteworthy, with my insights and view

AI-Assisted Skills Architecture Creation: A Game Changer

The new AI-assisted skills architecture creation tool is a standout feature that addresses a significant challenge many organizations face: starting a skill library from scratch. This tool leverages AI to analyse job role data and automatically suggests a relevant skills library, making it easier for organizations to initiate their journey with the Talent Intelligence Hub.

Key Points:
- Automated Skill Mapping: The tool focuses on job role description fields to determine necessary skills, streamlining the process of building a comprehensive skills library.
- AI Unit Requirement: Access to this feature requires licensed AI units, ensuring that only premium users benefit from this advanced capability.
- Industry-Specific Insights: Initially supporting a range of industries like software, manufacturing, and general business, SAP plans to expand its coverage, enhancing the tool's relevance across more sectors.

For those looking for a more tailored approach, TalenTeam’s TalentBox and Talent Intelligence Hub Accelerator offers personalized services to generate an AI-driven skills library that matches specific industry needs.

NextGen Career Development: Integrated and Intuitive

The integration of the Growth Portfolio with Career Development and the Opportunity Marketplace is a significant enhancement. It brings a holistic view of career opportunities directly within the Growth Portfolio, enabling users to see targeted roles, understand skill gaps, and access role-readiness assessments seamlessly.

Enhancements Include:
- Role and Opportunity Insights: Direct integration allows users to view role-specific insights and growth opportunities without navigating away from their current workflow.
- Opportunity Marketplace Integration: Users with access to the Opportunity Marketplace will see tailored recommendations, further personalizing their development experience.

In one of the sessions last year at SuccessConnect, the SAP Product managers mentioned that a user shouldn't need to go to a module to access information. Any SF module should present the right information to the user in the screen that they are in - this seems to bring that to life!

 

Streamlining Upgrades with New Tools

Upgrading from Centre of Capabilities to Growth Portfolio still remains a challenging process for many. The latest release introduces new tools and functionalities to ease this transition:
- Check Tool Updates: New tools address common upgrade issues, such as invalid skills and competencies and job family mismatches in the Job Description Manager, offering quick fixes to streamline the upgrade process.
- Enhanced Reporting Capabilities: This release also expands the reporting functionalities, allowing users to export competencies and skills from the Talent Intelligence Hub for enhanced data analysis and decision-making.

Report Stories

With this release, you can now export the Competencies and Skills available for reporting in conjunction with Job Profile Builder data, so we can report on all skills and
competencies mapped to specific roles

The 1H 2024 release of SAP SuccessFactors marks a significant step forward towards management of talent, leveraging advanced AI technologies and integration to enhance user experience and organizational efficiency. These tools will help organizations with a speedier path to a skills based organizations. As SAP continues to evolve, these enhancements are set to offer organizations the tools they need to thrive in an increasingly competitive business landscape.

entry.title: Re: What is the purpose of the Lesson Types in the Course...
entry.content:
Hi,

If you specify specific Simulation modes in an Assignment, only those modes are shown in the Learners View for the corresponding users.  In the image below, I created an Assignment that shows only Books and Demo Mode Simulations.

 If you open an actual Simulation, by clicking on a Simulation object, not the Demo Mode object, the Simulation view is shown and the user can access the other modes of the Simulation.  However, the Assignment only shows direct access and reporting of the Demo Mode Simulation to the user.

Regards
Shane

Unlock exclusive rewards today with the Linea referral code 'aMb3UVLbcZ'!

Join Linea, the ultimate platform for seamless communication and collaboration, and experience a whole new level of productivity and connectivity. Whether you're a freelancer, entrepreneur, or part of a team, Linea has everything you need to streamline your workflow and achieve your goals.

By using the referral code 'aMb3UVLbcZ' when signing up, you'll gain access to a range of exclusive rewards, including premium features, discounts on subscriptions, and much more. Share the code with your friends, colleagues, and networks to spread the benefits and enhance everyone's Linea experience.

Don't miss out on this opportunity to elevate your productivity and collaboration with Linea. Sign up today with the referral code 'aMb3UVLbcZ' and unlock a world of possibilities!

entry.title: Linea Referral Code 'aMb3UVLbcZ': Unlock Exclusive Rewards Today!
entry.content:
Unlocking exclusive rewards with Linea's referral code 'aMb3UVLbcZ' is simple! Just follow these easy steps:

  1. Sign up for Linea: Head to the Linea website or download the Linea app from your preferred app store.

  2. Enter the Referral Code: During the signup process, you'll be prompted to enter a referral code. Input 'aMb3UVLbcZ' in the designated field.

  3. Complete Registration: Fill out the required information to create your Linea account. Make sure to verify your email address if necessary.

  4. Enjoy Exclusive Rewards: Once your account is set up with the referral code, you'll automatically unlock exclusive rewards and benefits. These may include premium features, discounts, or other incentives.

By following these steps, you'll be on your way to enjoying all the perks Linea has to offer while maximizing your productivity and collaboration.

entry.title: Re: Ability to override display settings?
entry.content:
Hi Andrew,

Unfortunately not.  The Zoom settings that you mention are set at a Workarea level can cannot be modified for individual Books or Book Pages.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Is it possible, to hide a slide with macro actions fr...
entry.content:
Hi,

Yes, this is possible.  To do this, open your Simulation in Producer, click on the embedded Book Page object, and modify the properties of the Book Page so that they look like the image below.

 

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Query with Parameter failing
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm working on a complex query in SAP Business One that uses subqueries. However, I'm encountering an error when trying to use a dynamic parameter.

The query works perfectly when:

  • The parameter is hardcoded.

The problem arises when:

  • I try to make the parameter dynamic using the HANA syntax [%0].

Here's the working query (without the dynamic parameter):


SELECT
MFC."StartDate",
MFC."EndDate",
MFC."ForecastName",
T1."CardCode" AS "Business Partner Code",
T1."CardName" AS "Business Partner Name",
T2."U_Brand" AS "Brand",
MFC."ItemCode" AS "ItemCode",
MFC."Date" AS "Day Forecast",
MFC."ForecastQTY" AS "Forecast QTY",
MFC."SaleDate" AS "Invoice Date",
T3."Name" AS "Bill-to Country",
OTER."descript" AS "Territory",
T4."IndName" AS "Industry",
T5."GroupName" AS "Group",
T6."SlpName" AS "Sales Manager",
'' AS "Warehouse", -- leaving blank for now until we forecast by warehouse
T2."ItemName",
T1."Currency" AS "BP Currency", -- Business partner currency
MFC."Sales Value GBP" AS "Line Total GBP",
MFC."SoldQTY" AS "Invoice QTY"
FROM
(
SELECT
T0."U_BPCode" AS "CardCode",
T0."Code" AS "ForecastName",
T0."StartDate",
T0."EndDate",
T1."ItemCode",
T1."Date",
T1."Quantity" AS "ForecastQTY",
TSD."SaleDate",
TSD."QuantitySold" AS "SoldQTY",
TSD."Sales Value GBP"
FROM
DEV_SADP.OFCT T0
INNER JOIN DEV_SADP.FCT1 T1 ON T0."AbsID" = T1."AbsID"
LEFT JOIN
(
-- Sum up Sales Data
SELECT
SCT."CardCode",
SCT."ItemCode",
SCT."SaleDate",
SUM(SCT."QTYSold") AS "QuantitySold",
SUM(SCT."GBPSalesValue") AS "Sales Value GBP"
FROM
(
SELECT
T0."CardCode",
ADD_MONTHS(NEXT_DAY(LAST_DAY(T0."DocDate")), -1) AS "SaleDate",
T1."ItemCode",
SUM(T1."Quantity") AS "QTYSold",
SUM(T1."LineTotal") AS "GBPSalesValue"
FROM
OINV T0
INNER JOIN INV1 T1 ON T0."DocEntry" = T1."DocEntry"
WHERE
T0."CANCELED" = 'N'
GROUP BY
T0."CardCode",
ADD_MONTHS(NEXT_DAY(LAST_DAY(T0."DocDate")), -1),
T1."ItemCode"
UNION ALL
SELECT
T0."CardCode",
ADD_MONTHS(NEXT_DAY(LAST_DAY(T0."DocDate")), -1) AS "SaleDate",
T1."ItemCode",
SUM(T1."Quantity") * -1 AS "QTYSold",
SUM(T1."LineTotal") * -1 AS "GBPSalesValue"
FROM
ORIN T0
INNER JOIN RIN1 T1 ON T0."DocEntry" = T1."DocEntry"
WHERE
T0."CANCELED" = 'N'
GROUP BY
T0."CardCode",
ADD_MONTHS(NEXT_DAY(LAST_DAY(T0."DocDate")), -1),
T1."ItemCode"
) AS SCT
GROUP BY
SCT."CardCode",
SCT."ItemCode",
SCT."SaleDate"
) TSD ON T0."U_BPCode" = TSD."CardCode" AND T1."Date" = TSD."SaleDate" AND T1."ItemCode" = TSD."ItemCode"
) MFC
LEFT JOIN "OCRD" T1 ON MFC."CardCode" = T1."CardCode"
LEFT JOIN "OITM" T2 ON MFC."ItemCode" = T2."ItemCode"
LEFT JOIN "OCRY" T3 ON T1."Country" = T3."Code"
LEFT JOIN "OTER" ON T1."Territory" = OTER."territryID"
LEFT JOIN "OOND" T4 ON T1."IndustryC" = T4."IndCode"
LEFT JOIN "OCRG" T5 ON T1."GroupCode" = T5."GroupCode"
LEFT JOIN "OSLP" T6 ON T1."SlpCode" = T6."SlpCode"
LEFT JOIN "OTER" TERPAR ON OTER."parent" = TERPAR."territryID";

 

 

 

Thanks

 

#sbo #query #parameter

entry.title: Re: BUSINESS TRANSACTION IN SAP S/4HANA CLOUD, PUBLIC EDI...
entry.content: Hi @kdkr,

entry.title: Re: BUSINESS TRANSACTION IN SAP S/4HANA CLOUD, PUBLIC EDI...
entry.content:
I already checked it in CBC,... Is there a way i can create a document type and change its business transaction? Right now, I can only copy the standard doc type but can't edit or change the business transaction. 

Business transaction field is grayed and cannot be edited/change same with the account types allowed.

entry.title: Re: Brief Introduction to Machine Learning capabilities in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi, This article addresses ML/AI support for S4. How much of these can work with ECC?

Thanks  - ravi

entry.title: Blast Invitation Code '09Y5X': Earn More Points Today!
entry.content: Unlock more points and rewards with the Blast invitation code '09Y5X'! Join Blast, the ultimate platform for gaming enthusiasts, and take your gaming experience to the next level. Simply enter the invitation code '09Y5X' during sign-up to start earning points instantly. With Blast, you can discover new games, compete with friends, and redeem your points for exciting rewards. Don't miss out on this opportunity to boost your gaming journey. Sign up with the invitation code '09Y5X' today and start earning rewards like never before!
